sanskrit: देवान्भावयतानेन ते देवा भावयन्तु वः। परस्परं भावयन्तः श्रेयः परमवाप्स्यथ।।
transliteration: devān bhāvayatānena te devā bhāvayantu vaḥ | parasparaṃ bhāvayantaḥ śreyaḥ param avāpsyatha ||
literal_meaning: By this (sacrifice) nourish the gods; may those gods nourish you; nourishing one another, you will attain the highest good.
contextual_meaning: Psychologically, the verse describes a reciprocal feedback loop: when we invest psychic energy into the ‘inner gods’—archetypal powers that regulate mood, health, creativity—these powers feed us back with vitality and meaning; mutual nourishment yields optimal functioning.
psychological_state: Secure attachment to internal and external resources; sense of being held by the universe when one performs one’s part.
trigger_scenario: You schedule 20 min of morning meditation or journaling (your ‘sacrifice’). The rest of the day your mood is stable, ideas flow, and interactions feel supportive—an everyday illustration of mutual nourishment.
anchor_text: Feed your inner gods; let them feed you back.
